title: Connector for a flexible elongate member insertable into a body
abstract: Devices, systems, and methods for coupling an elongate member with a shaft of an endoscope. The device for coupling an elongate member with a shaft of an endoscope may include a first contoured portion configured to mate with an outer surface of the shaft and a second contoured portion configured to couple with the elongate member. When the first contoured portion is mated with the outer surface of the shaft and the second contoured portion is coupled with the elongate member, the elongate member may be secured relative to the shaft. When secured relative to the shaft, the elongate member may be able to move longitudinally relative to the shaft.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A device for coupling an elongate member with a shaft of an endoscope, the device comprising: a first contoured portion configured to mate with an outer surface of the shaft of the endoscope; and a second contoured portion configured to couple with the elongate member, and wherein when the first contoured portion is mated with the outer surface of the shaft and the second contoured portion is coupled with the elongate member, the elongate member is secured relative to the shaft and is adjustable relative to the shaft. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the second contoured portion is configured such that the elongate member is able to adjust rotationally and longitudinally relative to the shaft when the elongate member is coupled with the second contoured portion and the first contoured portion is mated with the outer surface of the shaft. 3. The device of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the second contoured portion is part of a coupling component configured to couple with the elongate member via a snap connection. 4. The device of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the first contoured portion is part of a coupling component configured to couple with the shaft via a snap connection. 5. The device of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the first contoured portion and the second contoured portion are monolithic. 6. The device of any one of claims 1-5, further comprising: a fixture defining the first contoured portion and the second contoured portion; and a band configured to engage the fixture and couple the fixture to the shaft of the endoscope. 7. The device of claim 6, wherein the band is configured to extend around the shaft and an outer surface of the fixture, the outer surface of the fixture faces a direction that is facing away from a surface of the first contoured portion. 8. The device of claim 6, wherein the band has a first end secured to the fixture and a second end secured to the fixture. 9. The device of any one of claims 1-8, further comprising: a band defining the first contoured portion; and a fixture configured to secure the band relative to the shaft of the endoscope, wherein the fixture defines the second contoured portion and a compartment configured to receive the band. 10. The device of claim 9, wherein the fixture includes a first sub-component and a second sub-component configured to couple with the first sub-component to define the compartment. 11. The device of any one of claims 1-10, further comprising: a band defining the first contoured portion; and a fixture comprising an opening configured to receive the band, and wherein the opening is located at one of a bottom of the fixture configured to face the shaft of the endoscope and a side of the fixture configured to face a direction parallel to a longitudinal axis of the shaft when the band is received within the fixture and the first contoured portion is mated with the shaft. 12. A system for coupling an elongate member with a shaft of an endoscope, the system comprising: a first coupling component; and a second coupling component configured to couple with the elongate member, and wherein: 

 the first coupling component is adjustable relative to the second coupling component to releasably couple with the shaft of the endoscope, and when the first coupling component is releasably coupled with the shaft and the second coupling component is coupled with the elongate member, the elongate member is secured relative to the endoscope and is able to move longitudinally relative to the shaft. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the first coupling component is annular and flexible, and the system further comprises: a delivery tool configured to expand the first coupling component to a diameter greater than a diameter of the shaft for delivery of the first coupling component and the second coupling component to a position along a length of the shaft. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein: the second coupling component has a groove in an outer surface, the groove is configured to receive the first coupling component, and the delivery tool has a groove in an outer surface, the groove is configured to receive the first coupling component. 15. A method of coupling an elongate member to a shaft of an endoscope, the method comprising: coupling a first coupling component to the shaft of the endoscope, wherein the elongate member is coupled to a second coupling component and coupling the first coupling component to the shaft secures the elongate member relative to the shaft at an axial location between a first end of the shaft and a second end of the shaft, and wherein the elongate member is able to move longitudinally relative to the second coupling component when coupled with the second coupling component and the first coupling component is coupled with the shaft.
